methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $135M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $600M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.1x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
SpliceBio is a Series B-stage biotech company that has raised $135M to date. Valuation analysis relies on a comparable universe of 4 same-stage private peers in Biotech & Health (Eikon Therapeutics, Neko Health, Asimov, Profluent), alongside 3 public sector comps, though public multiples cannot be directly applied due to absence of target revenue data.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression, using the $135M total raised as a proxy baseline and applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, implies a valuation of $256M. Peer-median analysis across the 4 private comparables yields $1.3B, with a stated range of $600M (Profluent) to $3.3B (Eikon Therapeutics). The wide dispersion reflects heterogeneity in technology maturity and capital efficiency within the cohort. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied due to the absence of SpliceBio's revenue figures, limiting reliance on the Biotech & Health sector P/S median of 6.1x.

Triangulation across both applicable methods supports a valuation range of $218M (low) to $1.5B (high), with a median of $778M. The wide band reflects material uncertainty around clinical/commercial stage, IP differentiation relative to peers, and burn trajectory. Stage-progression yields the conservative end; peer-median anchors toward the upper bound. Further specificity would require disclosure of R&D productivity metrics and anticipated financing milestones.
